title: [Task Sprint] QA Testing Report — Villume Admin

issues.main_description_html:
• Make the Login, Register, Forgot Password, and Reset Password pages Standalone.

QA Tester Summary - Issue #1527: [Standalone] Migrate Authentication Pages

Suite: qa_testing/tests/e2e/standalone/issue_1527_auth_pages_standalone.spec.ts (Playwright - Chromium)
Environment: Dev https://dev.villume.com/ with Access-Code header, HTTPS errors ignored
Overall Result: Failed / Open Bug

Test Scenario & Result

# Box Scenario Given -> Expected Matrix Result
1 White Auth source/component wiring Auth routes/components are reviewed -> forgot/reset routes lazy-load and login/register components are locally imported in layout dialog scope TP Pass
2 Black Forgot-password page render Visitor opens /forgot-password -> reset-request form renders and submit is blocked until required inputs/reCAPTCHA are satisfied TP Pass
3 Black Direct reset-password guard Visitor opens /reset-password without reset token -> app redirects to /404 instead of exposing reset form TP Pass
4 Gray Reset token handoff Visitor opens synthetic /resettoken?code=1527&token=qa-reset-token-1527 -> app resolves to /reset-password and renders set-new-password form TP Pass
5 Black Reset form validation User fills invalid/mismatched passwords -> inline validation appears and submit remains blocked TP Pass
6 Gray Reset submit contract Valid reset form submit is mocked -> request uses reset code/token and success UI renders without changing a real account TP Pass
7 Gray Forgot-password email delivery Real reset email should arrive after forgot-password request -> user reported no email received FN Fail

Bug Notes

  • Forgot-password email delivery was not received by the user on dev, so real reset email delivery could not be validated as passed.
  • This email-delivery issue is now treated as the blocking bug for this issue per QA direction.
  • Login/Register route validation was skipped per QA direction because the current implementation exposes login/register through layout dialog components, not dedicated /login or /register page routes.
  • Reset-password success was validated with a synthetic token and mocked API response to avoid changing a real account password.

Code Review

Area Reference Review
Auth routes src/app/routes/auth.routes.ts /forgot-password, /reset-password, and /resettoken are wired with loadComponent to their intended page components.
Login/Register components src/app/layouts/login/login.component.ts, src/app/layouts/sign-up/sign-up.component.ts, src/app/layouts/layouts.component.ts Login and Register are component/dialog based in the layout shell; route-level validation was skipped by QA direction.
Forgot password page src/app/pages/forgot-password/forgot-password.component.ts Component declares local imports and renders the reset-request form with reCAPTCHA dependency.
Reset token handoff src/app/pages/reset-password/reset-token/reset-token.component.ts Query code and token are stored in ResetPasswordService, then browser navigation continues to /reset-password.
Reset password page src/app/pages/reset-password/reset-password.component.ts Form validates required fields, minimum length, password confirmation, and blocks direct access when no token is present.
Reset API service src/app/pages/reset-password/reset-password.service.ts Submit sends reset code, new password fields, and Authorization: Bearer <token>.

Response Code Review

Request Expected Actual Result
GET /forgot-password Public auth page returns 200 OK and renders forgot-password form Page rendered the reset-request form Pass
Browser /reset-password without token User should not see reset form without token App redirected to /404 Pass
Browser /resettoken?code=1527&token=qa-reset-token-1527 Handoff should open /reset-password form Set-new-password form rendered Pass
Mocked PATCH /v1/auth/password Request should include code, password fields, and bearer token Mock captured expected payload and token Pass
Real forgot-password email delivery Reset email should be received by user User reported no email received Fail

White / Gray / Black Box Coverage

Box Coverage Result
White Box Reviewed auth route declarations, login/register dialog components, forgot/reset components, token handoff, and reset API service. Pass for migration scope
Gray Box Used synthetic reset token and mocked reset submit to validate handoff/API contract without changing a real account. Pass
Black Box Opened forgot-password route, verified reset guard behavior, checked reset form validation and success UI. Email delivery remains failed/not received. Fail on email delivery

Confusion Matrix

Classification Meaning Count
TP Expected behavior correctly passed 6
TN Expected negative behavior correctly rejected 0
FP Test reported a failure where behavior was acceptable 0
FN A required acceptance path is present but fails for users 1

Evidence

  • Screenshot: issue-1527-forgot-password-route.png
  • Screenshot: issue-1527-reset-password-without-token-guard.png
  • Screenshot: issue-1527-reset-token-form.png
  • Screenshot: issue-1527-reset-password-validation.png
  • Screenshot: issue-1527-reset-password-mocked-success.png
  • Screenshot: issue-1527-video-success-frame.png
  • Video: issue-1527-auth-reset-addressbar.mp4

Final QA Decision

Issue #1527 should remain open as a bug. The migration/rendering scope passed, but forgot-password email delivery was not received on dev. Apply labels Bug and Sanity Testing, and assign to ekosy9.

Screenshots

Forgot-password route renders the reset-request form; real email delivery is noted as not received:

Mocked reset submit reaches Password updated without changing a real account:

Reset-password inline validation blocks mismatched passwords:

Direct /reset-password without token is guarded and redirects to /404:

Synthetic /resettoken link opens the /reset-password form:

Video frame showing address bar on /reset-password during reset validation:

Video Recording

Address-bar recording covering forgot-password route, reset guard, synthetic reset token, validation, and mocked success:

issue-1527-auth-reset-addressbar.mp4

Epic: Foundation (integration)

Reimplement the static site's scroll-reveal animation as an Angular directive.

Scope

  • revealOnScroll directive using IntersectionObserver.
  • SSR no-op (guard with isPlatformBrowser); elements visible by default if JS/observer unavailable.
  • Replaces the .reveal behavior from assets/app.js.

Acceptance criteria

  • Elements animate in on scroll in the browser.
  • Server-rendered HTML shows content (no hidden content if JS disabled).

Testing scenario (QA)

  1. Scroll down a marketing page → sections fade/slide in as they enter the viewport.
  2. Disable JavaScript → all content is still visible (not stuck hidden).
  3. View page source (SSR) → content present in HTML.

Demo by Developer

2026-06-05.14-49-09.mp4

QA Tester Summary - Issue #1557: revealOnScroll directive (SSR-safe)

Suite: qa_testing/tests/e2e/foundation/issue_1557_reveal_on_scroll_ssr_safe.spec.ts (Playwright - Chromium)
Environment: Dev https://dev.villume.com/ with Access-Code header, HTTPS errors ignored
Overall Result: Failed

Test Scenario & Result

# Box Scenario Given -> Expected Matrix Result
1 Black Scroll reveal on marketing page User scrolls down / -> below-fold marketing sections fade/slide into view TP Pass
2 Black JavaScript disabled fallback JS disabled on / -> all .reveal content remains visible, not stuck hidden FN Fail
3 Gray SSR/page source content Request marketing route HTML -> meaningful page content is present before hydration TP Pass
4 White Architecture check Verify shared Angular revealOnScroll directive implementation - Skipped per confirmation

Bug Notes

  • With JavaScript disabled, below-fold home page sections remain visually hidden. The page renders the hero, then large blank areas appear where .reveal sections should be visible.
  • CSS currently sets .villume-home .reveal to opacity: 0 and transform: translateY(22px) until .in is added. When JavaScript is disabled, no client script can add .in, so the fallback acceptance criterion is not met.
  • A short dev-environment instability was also observed during rerun: curl -k -I --max-time 20 https://dev.villume.com/ timed out once, and a longer request returned 504 Gateway Time-out. The failure above is still reproducible from the captured no-JS screenshot and CSS behavior.

Code Review

Area Reference Review
Home reveal initialization src/app/layouts/main/main.component.ts Uses component-level IntersectionObserver in initReveal() and adds .in when entries intersect. Shared directive verification was skipped by request.
Home reveal CSS src/app/layouts/main/main.component.scss .reveal defaults to hidden (opacity: 0, translated down) and only becomes visible with .reveal.in. This causes the no-JS fallback bug.
Reduced motion fallback src/app/layouts/main/main.component.scss prefers-reduced-motion: reduce makes reveal content visible, but this only helps users with that media setting and does not solve general JS-disabled fallback.
Other marketing pages src/app/pages/about, catalogue, contact, privacy, service Reveal behavior is still handled in page/component logic. Architecture validation was not counted in QA result.

Response Code Review

Request Expected Actual Result
GET / in browser with access header Home page loads and content is available Home page loaded for evidence capture Pass
GET / with JavaScript disabled Content remains visible without hydration/reveal script Hero visible, below-fold reveal sections hidden/blank Fail
curl -k -I --max-time 20 / Stable 200 OK from dev environment Timed out during one rerun Environment note

White / Gray / Black Box Coverage

Box Coverage Result
White Box Reviewed reveal CSS and component implementation relevant to the no-JS fallback. Shared directive requirement was intentionally skipped. Fail finding in CSS fallback
Gray Box Verified SSR/page content is present and not just an empty shell based on rendered snapshot/evidence. Pass
Black Box Scrolled the home marketing page and tested the same page with JavaScript disabled. Fail on no-JS fallback

Confusion Matrix

Classification Meaning Count
TP Expected behavior correctly passed 2
TN Expected negative behavior correctly rejected 0
FP Test reported a failure where behavior was acceptable 0
FN A required acceptance path is present but fails for users 1

Evidence

  • Screenshot: issue-1557-scroll-reveal-in-view.png
  • Screenshot: issue-1557-js-disabled-hidden-reveals.png
  • Screenshot: issue-1557-video-js-disabled-hidden-reveals.png
  • Video: issue-1557-dev-addressbar-clean.mp4

Final QA Decision

Issue #1557 should remain open as a bug. Apply labels Bug and Sanity Testing, and assign to ekosy9.

Screenshots

JavaScript disabled - below-fold reveal sections are hidden/blank:

Scroll reveal - below-fold section visible after scrolling:

Video frame - Chrome address bar on https://dev.villume.com:

Video frame - home page loaded in Chrome with address bar:

JavaScript disabled full-page evidence from address-bar recording flow:

Focused scroll reveal element screenshot:

Video Recording

issue-1557-dev-addressbar-clean.mp4

• Make the Contact, About, Careers, Privacy, Support, and FAQ pages Standalone.

QA Tester Summary - Issue #1532: [Standalone] Migrate Static / Other Pages

Suite: qa_testing/tests/e2e/standalone/issue_1532_static_other_pages_standalone.spec.ts (Playwright - Chromium)
Environment: Dev https://dev.villume.com/ with Access-Code header, HTTPS errors ignored
Overall Result: Passed

Test Scenario & Result

# Box Scenario Given -> Expected Matrix Result
1 White Static route source wiring Routes for /contact, /about, /careers, and /privacy-cookie-policy -> each uses lazy loadComponent for the intended page component TP Pass
2 Black Public static pages render Visitor opens each public static page -> HTTP 200, meaningful heading/content, and no 404 fallback TP Pass
3 White FAQ scope as component-only No /faq route is expected per QA direction -> FaqComponent exists and renders FAQ accordion semantics TP Pass
4 Black Guarded support page Support-role account logs in and opens /support -> support Clients page renders instead of redirecting to /404 TP Pass

Bug Notes

  • No bug found in the confirmed QA scope.
  • Explicit standalone: true verification was skipped per QA direction. This run validates lazy component route behavior and rendered page access.
  • /faq page route was not required. FAQ was validated as a component/section target only.

Code Review

Area Reference Review
Static routes src/app/routes/static.routes.ts /contact, /careers, /about, and /privacy-cookie-policy are wired with loadComponent to their page components.
Support route src/app/routes/app-features.routes.ts /support is wired with loadComponent and protected by authSupportGuard.
FAQ component src/app/components/faq/faq.component.ts, src/app/components/faq/faq.component.html FAQ exists as FaqComponent, imports its required module locally, and renders an accessible FAQ region/accordion.
Support guard src/app/shared/route-guards/auth-support.guard.ts Guard allows support users and redirects non-support/unauthenticated users to /404, matching the protected support-page behavior.

Response Code Review

Request Expected Actual Result
GET /contact Public static page returns 200 OK and renders Contact content Contact page rendered with Contact Us content Pass
GET /about Public static page returns 200 OK and renders About content About page rendered with mission/about content Pass
GET /careers Public static page returns 200 OK and renders Careers content Careers page rendered with no-open-positions content Pass
GET /privacy-cookie-policy Public static page returns 200 OK and renders Privacy content Privacy page rendered with legal/privacy content Pass
Browser /support after support login Support-role account can access the guarded Clients page /support rendered the Clients table with support navigation/user visible Pass

White / Gray / Black Box Coverage

Box Coverage Result
White Box Reviewed route declarations and component targets for static pages, support, and FAQ component-only scope. Pass
Gray Box Verified public route responses through Playwright request checks and matched rendered content markers. Pass
Black Box Opened public pages in browser, logged in with support-role account, and validated guarded /support rendering. Pass

Confusion Matrix

Classification Meaning Count
TP Expected behavior correctly passed 4
TN Expected negative behavior correctly rejected 0
FP Test reported a failure where behavior was acceptable 0
FN A required acceptance path is present but fails for users 0

Evidence

  • Screenshot: issue-1532-contact-standalone-page.png
  • Screenshot: issue-1532-about-standalone-page.png
  • Screenshot: issue-1532-careers-standalone-page.png
  • Screenshot: issue-1532-privacy-standalone-page.png
  • Screenshot: issue-1532-faq-component-section.png
  • Screenshot: issue-1532-support-authenticated-page.png
  • Screenshot: issue-1532-video-support-frame.png
  • Video: issue-1532-static-other-pages-addressbar.mp4

Final QA Decision

Issue #1532 is ready to close as Passed. Apply labels Passed and Unit Testing, and assign to ekosy9.

Careers route rendered as standalone/lazy component page:

Contact route rendered as standalone/lazy component page:

FAQ component/section evidence, with no /faq route required:

Privacy route rendered as standalone/lazy component page:

issues.display_title: Issue #1555: [Foundation] Self-host Geist / Geist Mono fonts
issues.main_description_html:
Epic: Foundation (design system)

Self-host the Geist and Geist Mono fonts used by the new design.

Scope

  • Add self-hosted Geist / Geist Mono web fonts (woff2) with font-display: swap.
  • Wire into the marketing typography tokens.
  • Google Fonts only as fallback.

Acceptance criteria

  • Fonts load on marketing pages without layout shift (CLS) issues.
  • SSR build serves fonts correctly (no FOUT/blocking).

Testing scenario (QA)

  1. Load /about on a throttled (Slow 3G) connection — text renders with fallback then swaps to Geist; no blank text.
  2. Verify in DevTools > Network that fonts are served from our domain (self-hosted), not blocked.
  3. Check CLS in Lighthouse is within budget on marketing pages.

QA Tester Summary - Issue #1555: Self-host Geist / Geist Mono fonts

Suite: qa_testing/tests/e2e/foundation/issue_1555_self_hosted_geist_fonts.spec.ts (Playwright - Chromium)
Environment: Dev https://dev.villume.com/ with Access-Code header, HTTPS errors ignored
Overall Result: Passed

Test Scenario & Result

# Box Scenario Given -> Expected Matrix Result
1 White Font files and SCSS wiring Geist / Geist Mono .woff2 files exist -> @font-face references them with font-display: swap TP Pass
2 Gray Self-hosted font asset responses Request /assets/fonts/geist/*.woff2 -> each font returns HTTP 200 and valid font/woff2 response TP Pass
3 Black Marketing page font rendering Load /about -> hero text remains visible and computed font resolves to Geist TP Pass
4 Black Layout stability during font load Load /about -> CLS stays within 0.1 budget TP Pass
5 Gray Google Fonts fallback handling Google Fonts remains present -> accepted as fallback per QA direction, not counted as failure - Skipped as blocker

Bug Notes

  • None found within the confirmed scope.
  • Google Fonts links are still present in src/index.html, but this was explicitly accepted as fallback for this QA run.
  • Legacy external font requests outside the Geist scope were not counted as failures because issue #1555 specifically validates Geist / Geist Mono self-hosting.

Code Review

Area Reference Review
Self-hosted font declarations src/assets/scss/common/_geist-fonts.scss Defines Geist weights 300/400/500/600/700 and Geist Mono weights 400/500 using local /assets/fonts/geist/*.woff2 files.
Font loading behavior src/assets/scss/common/_geist-fonts.scss Each Geist / Geist Mono @font-face declaration uses font-display: swap, matching the acceptance requirement.
Marketing typography tokens src/assets/scss/common/_marketing-typography.scss --font-sans resolves to Geist; --font-mono resolves to Geist Mono.
Font files src/assets/fonts/geist/ Required Geist and Geist Mono .woff2 files are present and non-empty.
Google Fonts fallback src/index.html Google Fonts links remain present and were treated as allowed fallback per QA direction.

Response Code Review

Request Expected Actual Result
GET /about in browser with access header About page loads and text is visible Hero text loaded and remained visible Pass
GET /assets/fonts/geist/Geist-Light.woff2 200 OK, font/woff2, non-empty body 200, font/woff2, 34,104 bytes Pass
GET /assets/fonts/geist/Geist-Regular.woff2 200 OK, font/woff2, non-empty body 200, font/woff2, 33,400 bytes Pass
GET /assets/fonts/geist/Geist-Medium.woff2 200 OK, font/woff2, non-empty body 200, font/woff2, 34,716 bytes Pass
GET /assets/fonts/geist/Geist-SemiBold.woff2 200 OK, font/woff2, non-empty body 200, font/woff2, 35,292 bytes Pass
GET /assets/fonts/geist/Geist-Bold.woff2 200 OK, font/woff2, non-empty body 200, font/woff2, 35,484 bytes Pass
GET /assets/fonts/geist/GeistMono-Regular.woff2 200 OK, font/woff2, non-empty body 200, font/woff2, 14,712 bytes Pass
GET /assets/fonts/geist/GeistMono-Medium.woff2 200 OK, font/woff2, non-empty body 200, font/woff2, 15,304 bytes Pass

White / Gray / Black Box Coverage

Box Coverage Result
White Box Reviewed font files, @font-face declarations, font-display: swap, and typography token wiring. Pass
Gray Box Verified direct font asset responses from /assets/fonts/geist/ and confirmed all required files return valid font responses. Pass
Black Box Loaded /about, verified visible hero text, computed Geist font family, and CLS within budget. Pass

Confusion Matrix

Classification Meaning Count
TP Expected behavior correctly passed 4
TN Expected negative behavior correctly rejected 0
FP Test reported a failure where behavior was acceptable 0
FN A required acceptance path is present but fails for users 0

Evidence

  • Screenshot: issue-1555-clear-about-rendered.png
  • Screenshot: issue-1555-clear-font-evidence-table.png
  • Video: issue-1555-clear-addressbar-font-evidence.mp4

Final QA Decision

Issue #1555 passed. Keep labels Passed and Unit Testing, assign to ekosy9, and keep the issue closed.

Rendered /about page with Geist marketing typography:

Address-bar video showing /about, the font evidence table, and the return to /about:

issues.display_title: Issue #1558: [Foundation][QA] SSR build verification for design-system foundation
issues.main_description_html:
Epic: Foundation (QA)

Verify the SSR build still renders correctly after the design-system foundation lands.

Scope

  • Run npm run build:ssr and serve; confirm marketing + product routes render server-side.
  • Catch any browser-API-at-load regressions introduced by foundation work.

Acceptance criteria

  • build:ssr succeeds; /, /about, /catalogue, /contact and key product routes render via SSR.

Testing scenario (QA)

  1. Run the SSR production build - completes without errors.
  2. curl / view-source each marketing route - meaningful HTML returned (not just an empty app shell).
  3. Load each route in the browser - hydrates without console errors.

QA Tester Summary - Issue #1558: SSR build verification for design-system foundation

Suite: qa_testing/tests/e2e/build/issue_1558_ssr_build_verification.spec.ts (Playwright - Chromium)
Env: Local SSR production build served at http://localhost:4200 from dist/villume-admin; visual evidence recorded on https://dev.villume.com
Build Command: npm.cmd run build:ssr
Confirmed Scope: /, /about, /catalogue, /contact, /help, /privacy-cookie-policy, and /term-of-service
Skipped: /pricing is not part of the available route scope for this task.

Test Scenario & Result

# Box Scenario Expected Result
1 White Review the SSR route implementation and test route markers Routes used by the test have route-specific SSR markers and no pricing dependency Pass
2 Gray Run the SSR production build build:ssr completes without errors and generates dist/villume-admin Pass
3 Gray Request SSR HTML for the confirmed routes HTTP 200 with meaningful route-specific HTML, not an empty app shell Pass
4 Black Load the confirmed routes in the browser and wait for hydration Route content remains visible after hydration Pass
5 Task Sprint Record clean address-bar evidence for the scoped routes Fullscreen desktop video shows browser address bar and scoped route transitions Pass

Response Code Review

Route HTTP Status SSR HTML Length Route Marker Found Result
/ 200 113,447 Yes Pass
/about 200 101,635 Yes Pass
/catalogue 200 89,621 Yes Pass
/contact 200 102,577 Yes Pass
/help 200 98,504 Yes Pass
/privacy-cookie-policy 200 107,389 Yes Pass
/term-of-service 200 110,579 Yes Pass

White, Gray, Black Box Coverage

Box Coverage Validation Result
White Box Reviewed route-level expectations, SSR markers, and excluded unavailable pricing scope Confirmed test routes align with the issue acceptance scope and available public routes Pass
Gray Box Executed the production SSR build and direct SSR HTML checks Build completed and route HTML includes meaningful content beyond an empty app shell Pass
Black Box Loaded scoped routes in a real browser after SSR serve User-visible route content remains present after hydration Pass

Confusion Matrix

Case Expected Classification Actual Classification Matrix Result
Confirmed SSR route returns 200 with route-specific HTML Positive Positive True Positive Pass
Confirmed SSR route hydrates and keeps expected visible content Positive Positive True Positive Pass
Empty app shell or missing route marker Negative Negative True Negative Pass
/pricing route check Out of scope Skipped Not Applicable Pass

Code Review Notes

  • The SSR build entry point generated dist/villume-admin/server/server.mjs and served successfully on http://localhost:4200.
  • The tested public routes returned server-rendered route-specific HTML with expected content markers.
  • The Playwright spec was adjusted so /pricing is not treated as a product route for this issue after scope confirmation.
  • No code defect was found within the confirmed scope.

Evidence

  • Screenshots: final scoped SSR route screenshots for /, /about, /catalogue, and /contact.
  • Video: clean fullscreen desktop recording with the browser address bar visible for the scoped SSR route flow.

Overall Result

Passed - SSR build, SSR response validation, scoped browser hydration, and clean address-bar evidence passed for the confirmed task scope.

SSR route screenshot - /about:

SSR route screenshot - /catalogue:

SSR route screenshot - /contact:

SSR route screenshot - /:

Video Recording

Clean Chrome address-bar recording on https://dev.villume.com:
